verdicts.title: Friend-invite strips the new companion's linkedUserId server-side (upsert fires before the member row exists) → 'Pending' badge silently reverts to 'Unlinked' and a duplicate chip appears when they accept
verdicts.reasoning: companions.ts:368-369 fires `void upsertTrip(trip)` (un-awaited, carries the fresh linkedUserId) BEFORE `await inviteTripMember`; upsert_trip runs _cleaned_companions (trips.py:254 + 42-62) which strips any linkedUserId not yet in trip_members, the read path (data.py:1055/1121) never re-links companions to members, and pullFromServer overwrites trip.companions wholesale (api.ts:147-149) while buildRosterChips dedupes only on linkedUserId (TripBody.tsx:802) — a genuine race that reverts the link and renders a duplicate chip, but no data loss (name + member row + ghost-roster balances survive), so P2 not P1.

verdicts.title: Invite failures (409 role-conflict, 404 blocked/unknown user, 403) are swallowed: companion is still linked + persisted and a false 'invited' success toast shows
verdicts.reasoning: inviteTripMember uses _post (core.ts:296-300) which .catch()es and returns the raw Response without throwing; companions.ts:369 ignores the result, sets the link optimistically + upserts (368), and unconditionally shows the success toast (372), so the server's 409 (trips.py:899-903) and 404 blocked/unknown (trips.py:869-876) never surface.

verdicts.title: Budgeteers are mislabelled as 'Relaxer' on the trip header role badge
verdicts.reasoning: TripBody.tsx:647-654 renders the hardcoded '👁 Relaxer' badge whenever `!tripIsEditable`, and tripIsEditable=canEdit (line 138) is planner-only (permissions.ts:49-51), so a budgeteer hits the relaxer branch with no budgeteer case — confirmed, but it's a cosmetic self-badge mislabel with no functional/permission impact (the chip panel labels budgeteers correctly at TripBody.tsx:928-936), hence P3.

verdicts.title: Linking a friend to a same-named existing unlinked companion silently merges two different people
verdicts.reasoning: companions.ts (modal) :360-362 matches the friend's name case-insensitively via findTripCompanion (companions.ts:45-57) and sets existingByName.linkedUserId with no identity check, and addTripCompanion dedupes by name (companions.ts:146-150) so two same-name rows can't coexist — real but an edge case requiring a name collision, no data loss, so P3.

verdicts.title: Companion remove-lock is case-sensitive while the rest of the companion system is case-insensitive — a referenced companion can lose its 🔒 and be removed
verdicts.reasoning: referencedNames is a raw Set of e.who/split keys (modals/companions.ts:69-74) tested with exact `referencedNames.has(c.name)` (line 93) while findTripCompanion/clean_companions are case-insensitive, so a casing mismatch ('alice' vs 'Alice') bypasses the lock — confirmed, but balance math uses a ghost-roster union so no money is lost (only the user-facing guard is skipped), hence P3.

verdicts.title: Non-owner Planner can invite and kick other members via the API even though the UI presents roster management as owner-only
verdicts.reasoning: can_edit_trip returns true for any accepted 'planner' role (helpers.py:205-210), so the invite gate (trips.py:856) and non-self remove gate (trips.py:1099, owner protected at 1101) admit non-owner planners while the frontend gates on isOwner (permissions.ts:64-79) — a genuine layer disagreement, but the actor is an owner-granted trusted planner and the owner is protected, so P3.
